Key Focus for nsso / What it Means for You
column5: Additional Advice

Short Description: The fund will request access to a secure digital folder containing all key company information.
Status: Your focus is on being meticulously organised. This is your chance to make a strong first impression of professionalism and preparedness.
column5: A well-structured data room that preemptively answers questions tells a story. Use it to reinforce your narrative of clarity and cohesion.

Short Description: The fund will validate your market size claims and the fundamental need for your product.
Status: You need to prove that the problem you're solving—digital identity fragmentation—is a significant and growing pain point.
column5: Frame nsso within the booming creator economy and the future of work. Use stats on the rise of solopreneurship and personal branding as proof of a massive, growing market.

Short Description: They will analyze your direct and indirect competitors (e.g., Linktree, Beacons, personal website builders).
Status: Your focus must be on clearly articulating how nsso is fundamentally different and not just an incremental improvement.
column5: Leverage your unique synergy. Frame competitors as simple "lists of links," while nsso is a dynamic "identity platform" that integrates a portfolio (EXP) and e-commerce (EMPORIUM), placing you in a new, more valuable category.

Short Description: They will scrutinise your strategy for attracting and retaining users.
Status: The focus is on demonstrating a clear, cost-effective plan to acquire the right kind of users who will become advocates for the platform.
column5: Highlight your exclusive, curated approach. The fact that you "exclusively vet all of them" creates a premium, high-quality network, which is a powerful differentiator that can lead to strong network effects and justify higher user lifetime value.

Short Description: You will give a full, in-depth demonstration of the nsso platform, walking through the user journey and key features.
Status: The goal is to show that the product is not just an idea but a functional, well-designed platform that delivers on its promises.
column5: Focus on the "most beautiful way to present yourself online." The glassmorphic design and slick UX are tangible assets. Show how nsso makes users feel "clear, but proud, of who you are."

Short Description: Their tech team may review your architecture for scalability, security, and quality.
Status: You must demonstrate that the technology is robust, secure, and capable of supporting future growth and features.
column5: Mention the vision for future-proofing, including the potential for Web3 integration where users can "own via WEB3." This shows forward-thinking and a larger technical vision beyond a simple web app.

Short Description: The fund will meticulously analyze your Excel model, stress-testing every assumption about user growth, pricing, and costs.
Status: Your focus is on defending your assumptions with logic and data. Show the 'why' behind the 'what' in your financial projections.
column5: Your model is stronger because it has multiple potential revenue streams: premium subscriptions for advanced features, and transaction fees from the EMPORIUM section. This diversification is highly attractive.

Short Description: The fund's lawyers will check that Coodeh Ltd. is properly incorporated, owns all nsso IP, and has no outstanding legal issues.
Status: The goal is to prove the company is "clean" and that an investment in it is legally sound, without hidden liabilities.
column5: Your EIS Advance Assurance letter is your trump card. It proves you've already passed a level of HMRC scrutiny and are a well-prepared, tax-efficient investment vehicle.

Website: Team DD: Founder & Key Person Interviews
Short Description: The partners will conduct deep interviews with you and any key team members to assess expertise, resilience, and vision.
Status: The focus is on demonstrating that you are the right person to lead this company and that you have the determination to succeed.
column5: Your passion and the clear, consistent mission articulated in your pitch documents are your greatest assets. They are investing in your ability to "evolve identity" and help people "find their place."

Short Description: The Partner championing you will present nsso to the IC, which makes the final investment decision.
Status: Your job is to equip your internal champion with everything they need to successfully argue your case on your behalf.
column5: Provide your champion with a concise summary, key metrics, and a powerful vision statement. A short video demo can also be a powerful tool for the IC meeting.

Short Description: You will receive the official, non-binding Term Sheet outlining the core investment terms from the fund.
Status: Your immediate focus is to read the document carefully and identify the key economic and control terms. This is a major milestone.
column5: A strong performance during DD and a compelling vision give you leverage. If multiple funds are interested, you'll have even more negotiating power on the valuation.

Short Description: You formally engage your lawyer and any advisors to review the Term Sheet. Do not do this alone.
Status: The focus is on getting expert advice on what is "market standard" and understanding the long-term implications of each clause.
column5: A professional legal team signals that you are a serious founder and helps you negotiate from a position of knowledge and strength.

Short Description: With your lawyer, you will negotiate the key financial terms: valuation, investment amount, and the size of the employee option pool.
Status: The goal is to agree on a valuation that is fair, funds the company adequately, and is not overly dilutive to you and the existing team.
column5: Your detailed financial model and clear GTM strategy are your main tools here. Use them to justify your valuation and funding request.

Short Description: You will negotiate terms related to control, such as board seats and "protective provisions" (actions the company cannot take without investor consent).
Status: The focus is on retaining enough operational control to run your business effectively while giving the investor appropriate oversight.
column5: Demonstrating a strong, well-reasoned vision for the company's future can give investors the confidence to grant you more autonomy.

Website: Finalise and Sign the Term Sheet
Short Description: Once all points are agreed, a final version is drafted and signed by both you (for Coodeh Ltd.) and the fund.
Status: This signals a mutual commitment to proceed with the investment. While non-binding, it is a morally significant agreement.
column5: Signing the term sheet grants you a period of "exclusivity" or "no-shop," meaning you cannot negotiate with other investors. Ensure this period is a reasonable length (e.g., 30-45 days).

Short Description: Your lawyer and the fund's lawyer will have a call to agree on a timeline and a checklist for closing the deal.
Status: The focus is on creating a clear, shared project plan to get from the signed Term Sheet to the money in the bank as efficiently as possible.
column5: Being prepared for this call with your own list of questions and desired timelines shows you are organised and proactive, setting a professional tone.

Short Description: The fund's lawyers will convert the Term Sheet into long-form, legally binding documents (Shareholders' Agreement, Subscription Agreement).
Status: This is a detail-oriented phase. You must review every line with your lawyer to ensure it accurately reflects the agreed-upon Term Sheet.
column5: Your leverage here is being organised and responsive. The faster you can provide information and feedback, the faster the process moves.

Short Description: You will compile a formal letter that discloses any known risks or issues against the "warranties" (promises you make about the business).
Status: The focus is on being completely transparent. Hiding something at this stage can kill the deal and your reputation.
column5: Full transparency builds trust. A thorough and honest disclosure process shows you are a credible founder, setting a positive tone for the future board relationship.

Short Description: All final, execution-version documents are compiled into a single set, ready for signature.
Status: Your focus is on a final review of all documents with your lawyer to ensure there are no errors or last-minute changes.
column5: A smooth, error-free closing process demonstrates your attention to detail and operational competence.

Short Description: The final documents are sent to all parties for electronic signature (e.g., via DocuSign).
Status: The focus is on ensuring all required signatories (founders, investors, the company itself) sign in a timely manner.
column5: Having all signatories briefed and ready to sign quickly avoids frustrating last-minute delays.

Short Description: Your lawyer and the fund's lawyer will formally confirm that all conditions required for the deal to close (e.g., all signatures received) have been satisfied.
Status: This is a formal legal checkpoint before any money is transferred.
column5: Your organisational skills are key. Having everything in order ensures this is a quick and seamless confirmation.

Short Description: You will provide a formal document (often on company letterhead) with the full bank details for Coodeh Ltd.
Status: The focus is on 100% accuracy to prevent any transfer errors or delays. Double-check every number.
column5: Providing clear, correct information instills confidence and avoids unnecessary friction at the most critical moment.

Short Description: The fund wires the investment, and you confirm its arrival in your company's bank account, informing all lawyers.
Status: This is the moment the deal is officially "closed" and the transaction is complete.

Short Description: You will hold a formal board meeting to approve the share allotment and ratify all legal documents.
Status: This is a crucial governance step to ensure the entire transaction is properly recorded in the company's minutes.
column5: Efficiently handling this final legal step demonstrates operational excellence and reinforces the fund's decision to invest in you.

Short Description: Your lawyer will file the required SH01 form to officially register the new shares with the UK government.
Status: This must be done within one month of the share allotment. It is a legal requirement.

Website: Issue Share Certificates & Update Cap Table
Short Description: You will issue official share certificates to the fund and update your company's capitalization table (and any software like Carta or Ledgy).
Status: This provides the investor with their formal proof of ownership and ensures your records are accurate for future funding rounds.

Short Description: After 4 months of trading, you submit the EIS1 form to HMRC. Upon approval, you receive and distribute the EIS3 certificates.
Status: This is the final and most important step for your investors, as it allows them to claim their tax relief.
column5: Completing this promptly is crucial. Doing it well makes you a founder they would gladly back in future funding rounds.
